observations: All major repository components have been summarized with extracted docstrings and comments, providing a solid base for component documentation.
The evaluate_docs python tool is registered and ready to evaluate documentation coverage against rubric criteria of entrypoints, setup accuracy, component coverage, and hallucination avoidance.
The draft documentation plan covers four key documents: setup, usage, components, and API reference, with associated skills and tools clearly outlined.
No README exists, so the plan wisely focuses on foundational documentation for setup and usage, along with component summaries and API references from source code.
The composite tool extract_docstrings_and_comments is in place for component summary extraction and already applied to key modules.
Skills for analyzing module summaries, developing the documentation plan, and evaluating documentation quality are defined and linked appropriately.
Workflow steps are proposed to generate documentation drafts, run evaluation, then refine or finalize documentation accordingly.
The system is ready to move forward with actual documentation content generation in a subsequent evolution step, using the current plan, tools, and skills.
workflow: Generate documentation drafts for setup, usage, components, and API reference documents using the defined skills and composite tools.
Run evaluate_docs on the generated documentation drafts along with the repository profile and rubric to assess coverage and accuracy quality.
Refine the documentation plan, skills, or tools if documentation evaluation indicates insufficient coverage, accuracy, or hallucination issues.
Repeat generation and evaluation cycles until documentation meets the rubric threshold to be ready for finalization.

documents.purpose: Document setup commands, dependencies, and configuration environment variables to guide installing and running the project.
Detail the main usage flow and entrypoint behavior from main.py including how data fetching, scoring, draft generation, and notification are orchestrated.
Provide summaries and explanations of major components like fetchers, database, generator, notifier, and models based on extracted module docstrings.
Catalog public interfaces and classes with signatures and descriptions for developers to reference.
